a:r.a:t: The router feedback loop and worker scheduler are two operational subsystems that keep the system healthy at scale. The feedback loop in router/feedback_loop.py tracks whether routing decisions actually work. After each turn, it classifies the outcome as positive, neutral, or negative. Dissatisfaction detection uses regex to match phrases like 'try again', 'you missed', 'wrong', or 'rephrase' in follow-up messages. To avoid noise, it samples only 10 percent of neutral outcomes and caps at 25 reviews per tenant per day. All labeled data goes to JSONL review files — structured training data for quarterly router pattern retraining. The worker scheduler in runtime/worker_scheduler.py implements weighted-fair-queuing with three priority lanes. Urgent tasks like escalations and SLA-critical work get weight 8. Interactive tasks for normal chat get weight 3. Background batch work gets weight 1. Per-tenant token budgets prevent any single heavy user from starving everyone else — 24,000 tokens per minute with burst allowance up to 48,000. The token cost factor of 1.35x accounts for overhead in estimation. Preemption lets urgent jobs jump ahead of background work. Together, these subsystems ensure the router keeps improving and resources stay fairly distributed.
